message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to know why the horse-riding soldier enters the temple.
Looking at the video, specifically around 02:20-03:15, soldiers on horseback are chasing a wounded man through a camp and up the temple stairs.
At 03:45-04:10, a soldier on horseback enters the temple gate, confronting monks who are protecting the wounded man.
At 04:02-04:10, the soldier on horseback threatens the monks, stating that anyone protecting the man Commander Hou wants to kill is an accomplice.
This clearly indicates the soldier is pursuing the wounded man.
Options:
A. To eat - No evidence.
B. To rest - No evidence.
C. To pursue the wounded - Supported by the chase and confrontation.
D. To steal - No evidence.

Therefore, the correct option is C.
I will structure the evidence accordingly.

message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to know why the horse-riding soldier enters the temple.
Looking at the video, around 02:20, soldiers on horseback are seen chasing someone.
At 02:54, a soldier shouts "Come out, let him come out" while searching tents.
At 03:02, a wounded man is hiding.
At 03:12, soldiers are chasing the wounded man up the temple stairs.
At 03:45, a soldier on horseback enters the temple gate, chasing the wounded man who is being protected by monks.
At 04:02, the soldier on horseback says "The person Commander Hou wants to kill, whoever dares to protect him is an accomplice."
This clearly indicates the soldier is pursuing the wounded man.
Therefore, the reason is to pursue the wounded.
Option C matches this.
